assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table line 17: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED |'. The detailed analysis section (lines 65-88) explicitly identifies both additive signals (test_recommend_purls_dedup, 3 new functions in purl_simplify.rs) and reductive signals (removed test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic).

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 72: '**REMOVED**: `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` function (reductive signal) -- This function tested that qualifier variants were returned as separate entries with `repository_url=` present. The entire function (14 lines of test body) was deleted because qualifier-specific behavior no longer exists.'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 74: '**MODIFIED**: `test_recommend_purls_basic` -- assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=https://repo1.maven.org&type=jar"`) to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers (`"pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12"`). ... This is a relaxation of the assertion (reductive signal) because the old assertion was strictly more specific (checked exact qualifier string), while the new assertion checks a shorter, less constrained value.'

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 76-80 under 'New file `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`:' lists three ADDED functions each marked as additive signal: test_simplified_purl_no_version, test_simplified_purl_mixed_types, test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved.

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 73 under 'Modified file `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs`:': '**ADDED**: `test_recommend_purls_dedup` function (additive signal) -- New function that seeds two PURLs with identical versions but different qualifiers and asserts only one deduplicated entry is returned with `assert_eq!(body.items.len(), 1)`.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. The report describes reading diffs and file content (e.g., criterion-1.md quotes diff hunks with +/- prefixes) but does not describe making any code modifications. No code files appear in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents a summary table with verdicts and an Overall PASS result, but contains no merge commands, no gh pr merge invocations, and no indication the PR was merged. The report ends with the Test Change Classification Analysis section, presenting findings for review.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: Summary table at report.md line 17: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED |'. Detailed analysis at line 65: '## Test Change Classification Analysis' with subsection 'Structural Summary' (line 69) listing per-function additions/removals/modifications, and subsection 'Semantic Assessment' (line 82) explaining the combination of reductive and additive signals.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary references specific file content: exact function names (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers, test_recommend_purls_dedup), exact assertion strings ('pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12?repository_url=...'), assertion code ('assert_eq!(body.items.len(), 1)'), and line counts ('14 lines of test body'). Line 88 explicitly states: 'This analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content, not acceptance criteria.'
